component.name: Button
component.description: Triggers an action or navigation; the primary lever for user intent in editorial workflows.

useCases: Cancel or secondary action alongside primary (neutral)
Inline low-emphasis control where a filled button is too heavy (text)

commonPatterns.name: Page header primary action
commonPatterns.description: Single primary button pinned to the right of the 726px header, paired with a neutral or text button for Cancel.

commonPatterns.name: Destructive confirm
commonPatterns.description: Danger button inside a confirmation modal. Always requires a neutral Cancel alongside it.

commonPatterns.name: Inline row action
commonPatterns.description: Text variant inside a table row — no fill, no border, minimal visual weight.

antiPatterns.scenario: Two primary buttons on the same view
antiPatterns.reason: Breaks the one forest.500 moment per view rule — both compete for the primary action.
antiPatterns.alternative: Keep one primary; demote secondary actions to neutral or text variant.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using primary for navigation (e.g. 'Go to article')
antiPatterns.reason: Navigation is not a primary action — it doesn't commit or mutate state.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a text button or a link component.

antiPatterns.scenario: Danger button without a confirmation step
antiPatterns.reason: Destructive actions are irreversible — users must confirm intent.
antiPatterns.alternative: Open a confirmation modal or inline confirm/cancel pair before executing.

slots.label: Required — sentence-case text
slots.iconRight: Optional — 16px icon after label (e.g. chevron for split button)

interactions.hover: Background steps one shade darker along the scale
interactions.disabled: neutral.100 background, neutral.400 text, cursor not-allowed
interactions.loading: Label replaced by spinner; button width locked to prevent layout shift

accessibility.keyboardSupport: Enter and Space activate; Tab moves focus in/out
accessibility.screenReader: Icon-only buttons must carry aria-label; loading state must announce via aria-live

aiHints.context: Reach for Button whenever the user needs to trigger an action. Default to neutral variant when a primary already exists on the view. Use text variant inside list rows or anywhere a full button would dominate.
neverUseWhen: The action is navigation-only — use a link or text button styled as a link
The surface already has a primary button — use neutral or text instead
The label would exceed ~4 words — rethink the label before widening the button
